content: Feature flag pitfalls: (1) Reusing stale toggles — Knight Capital lost $500M from an old flag reactivated in new code; (2) No lifecycle management — flags without owners and expiration dates become permanent dead code; (3) Confusing release toggles (short-lived) with ops kill-switches (permanent) — release flags that never get removed; (4) Missing kill switch evaluation path — must evaluate without remote calls and default to OFF; (5) Inadequate access controls — non-technical users toggling flags without context; (6) No monitoring baseline before enabling — bugs at 5% rollout vs 100% rollout are very different; (7) Rollout % logic applied inconsistently — same user gets different flag value on refresh; (8) Flags never cleaned up — every flag is a branch that must be maintained.
